concepts.name: Creational
concepts.description: Design patterns that control, abstract, or centralize object creation so client code does not depend directly on concrete construction details.

subconcepts.name: Abstract factory
subconcepts.description: Provides an interface for creating families of related objects without requiring client code to know their concrete classes.

subconcepts.name: Builder
subconcepts.description: Separates the construction of a complex object from its final representation, often by accumulating configuration through a dedicated builder object.

subconcepts.name: Factory method
subconcepts.description: Defines a creation method that lets subclasses or implementations decide which concrete object to instantiate.

subconcepts.name: Prototype
subconcepts.description: Creates new objects by cloning or copying an existing prototype instance instead of constructing them from scratch.

subconcepts.name: Singleton
subconcepts.description: Ensures a class or component has a single shared instance and provides a controlled global access point to it.

subconcepts.name: Multiton
subconcepts.description: Maintains a controlled set of named or keyed shared instances, rather than a single global instance.

subconcepts.name: Object pool
subconcepts.description: Reuses expensive objects from a managed pool instead of repeatedly allocating and destroying them.

subconcepts.name: Dependency injection
subconcepts.description: Supplies a component's dependencies from the outside, usually through constructors, setters, providers, or a container, instead of letting the component construct them directly.

subconcepts.name: Lazy initialization
subconcepts.description: Defers creation of an object or expensive value until it is first needed, often hiding the creation behind an accessor or factory.

concepts.name: Structural
concepts.description: Design patterns that compose classes or objects into larger structures while keeping responsibilities, interfaces, and dependencies manageable.

subconcepts.name: Adapter
subconcepts.description: Wraps an object or interface so it can be used through another interface expected by client code.

subconcepts.name: Bridge
subconcepts.description: Separates an abstraction from its implementation so both can vary independently.

subconcepts.name: Composite
subconcepts.description: Represents part-whole hierarchies so clients can treat individual objects and object compositions uniformly.

subconcepts.name: Decorator
subconcepts.description: Adds behavior to an object by wrapping it with another object that preserves the same interface.

subconcepts.name: Facade
subconcepts.description: Provides a simplified interface over a larger subsystem or group of collaborators.

subconcepts.name: Flyweight
subconcepts.description: Shares immutable or reusable state between many fine-grained objects to reduce memory usage.

subconcepts.name: Proxy
subconcepts.description: Provides a placeholder or surrogate object that controls access to another object, often for lazy loading, remote access, caching, logging, or authorization.

subconcepts.name: Delegation
subconcepts.description: Forwards responsibility for part of an operation to another object, allowing behavior to be reused or varied through composition.

subconcepts.name: Module
subconcepts.description: Groups related data, functions, classes, or services behind a cohesive namespace or boundary with a clear public interface.

subconcepts.name: Extension object
subconcepts.description: Allows additional behavior or data to be attached to an object or hierarchy without modifying the original type hierarchy.

concepts.name: Behavioral
concepts.description: Design patterns that organize communication, responsibility, algorithms, or state-dependent behavior between objects or components.

subconcepts.name: Chain of responsibility
subconcepts.description: Passes a request through a chain of handlers until one handles it, reducing coupling between sender and receiver.

subconcepts.name: Command
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates a request as an object so it can be queued, logged, parameterized, retried, or undone.

subconcepts.name: Interpreter
subconcepts.description: Represents grammar rules and evaluates sentences or expressions in a small language or domain-specific notation.

subconcepts.name: Iterator
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates traversal of a collection or aggregate object without exposing its internal representation.

subconcepts.name: Mediator
subconcepts.description: Centralizes communication between collaborating objects so they do not depend directly on each other.

subconcepts.name: Memento
subconcepts.description: Captures an object's internal state so it can later be restored without exposing that state to other objects.

subconcepts.name: Null object
subconcepts.description: Provides a harmless object with default behavior to replace null checks and missing collaborators.

subconcepts.name: Observer
subconcepts.description: Defines a one-to-many dependency where observers are notified when a subject changes state.

subconcepts.name: Publish-subscribe
subconcepts.description: Decouples message producers from consumers through topics, events, channels, or a broker-like intermediary.

subconcepts.name: Specification
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates business rules or predicates as composable objects that can be combined, reused, and tested independently.

subconcepts.name: State
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates state-specific behavior in separate objects so an object changes behavior when its internal state changes.

subconcepts.name: Strategy
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates interchangeable algorithms or policies behind a common interface so the client can choose or swap behavior.

subconcepts.name: Template method
subconcepts.description: Defines the skeleton of an algorithm in a base operation while deferring selected steps to subclasses or hooks.

subconcepts.name: Visitor
subconcepts.description: Separates operations from the object structure they operate on, allowing new operations to be added without changing the visited classes.

subconcepts.name: Blackboard
subconcepts.description: Coordinates independent knowledge sources through a shared data structure where partial solutions are posted and consumed.

concepts.name: Concurrency
concepts.description: Design patterns that structure asynchronous, concurrent, or parallel work at the component level rather than merely using low-level synchronization primitives.

subconcepts.name: Active object
subconcepts.description: Decouples method invocation from execution by queueing requests and processing them asynchronously in a separate execution context.

subconcepts.name: Balking
subconcepts.description: Refuses to execute an operation when an object is not in an appropriate state, often returning immediately instead of blocking.

subconcepts.name: Guarded suspension
subconcepts.description: Suspends an operation until a required condition becomes true, usually while coordinating access to shared state.

subconcepts.name: Monitor object
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates shared state and synchronizes access through methods protected by mutual exclusion.

subconcepts.name: Read-write lock
subconcepts.description: Separates shared read access from exclusive write access so multiple readers can proceed concurrently while writers remain exclusive.

subconcepts.name: Reactor
subconcepts.description: Demultiplexes events from one or more sources and dispatches them to registered handlers, commonly for non-blocking I/O.

subconcepts.name: Scheduler
subconcepts.description: Centralizes decisions about when tasks, jobs, callbacks, or threads are allowed to execute.

subconcepts.name: Thread pool
subconcepts.description: Maintains a reusable set of worker threads that pull tasks from a queue, avoiding unbounded thread creation.

subconcepts.name: Thread-specific storage
subconcepts.description: Associates data with a particular thread so each thread sees its own isolated instance of otherwise global or shared-looking state.

subconcepts.name: Producer-consumer
subconcepts.description: Separates components that produce work items from components that consume them, usually through a queue or buffer.

subconcepts.name: Future or promise
subconcepts.description: Represents a result that may become available later, allowing asynchronous work to be composed, awaited, or chained.

subconcepts.name: Join pattern
subconcepts.description: Coordinates multiple concurrent messages or tasks by triggering behavior only when a required combination of events has occurred.

concepts.name: Architectural
concepts.description: Design patterns that organize larger application boundaries, responsibilities, persistence, presentation, or communication between layers and components.

subconcepts.name: Model-view-controller
subconcepts.description: Separates domain data, user interface rendering, and input/control logic into model, view, and controller responsibilities.

subconcepts.name: Model-view-viewmodel
subconcepts.description: Separates UI representation from presentation state and behavior through a view model that exposes data and commands to the view.

subconcepts.name: Repository
subconcepts.description: Provides a collection-like abstraction over data access, hiding persistence details from domain or application logic.

subconcepts.name: Unit of work
subconcepts.description: Tracks changes to objects during a business transaction and coordinates committing or rolling them back as a single unit.

subconcepts.name: Data access object
subconcepts.description: Encapsulates persistence operations for a data source or entity, separating storage-specific code from business logic.

subconcepts.name: Service layer
subconcepts.description: Defines application operations as services that coordinate domain logic, persistence, transactions, and external integrations.

subconcepts.name: Front controller
subconcepts.description: Routes all incoming requests through a single entry point that dispatches to the appropriate handlers or controllers.

subconcepts.name: Event bus
subconcepts.description: Provides a shared event dispatch mechanism that lets producers publish events without depending directly on consumers.

subconcepts.name: Command query responsibility segregation
subconcepts.description: Separates operations that change state from operations that read state, often using different models or handlers for commands and queries.
